mnemonics: Posterior is proportional to likelihood and prior
Bayes Theorem = P(A|B) = P(B|A) * P(A) / P(B)
keyPoints: Bayes Theorem is used for updating probabilities based on new evidence
Likelihood and prior are used to calculate posterior probability
The probability of A given B (P(A|B)) is equal to the probability of B given A (P(B|A)) times the prior probability of A times the inverse of the prior probability of B
